description:
You are the new binary-artifact curator.  
    Set up an hourly housekeeping job that archives old files from an “incoming”
    repository into a date-stamped sub-folder, and keeps a human-readable audit
    trail.  Everything must live inside /home/user, you do NOT need root.

    1. Directory layout  
       • /home/user/artifacts/incoming      – place where other teams drop new binaries  
       • /home/user/artifacts/archive       – top-level archive folder (will contain YYYY-MM sub-folders)  
       • /home/user/artifacts/logs          – log area  
       • /home/user/scripts                 – keep operational scripts here  
       • /home/user/cron.d                  – per-user cron snippets (this directory may not exist yet)  

    2. Archiving script  
       Create /home/user/scripts/archive_old_artifacts.sh with the exact she-bang  
           #!/usr/bin/env bash  
       Functionality requirements:  
       a) Runtime variable  
          TARGET_ARCHIVE_DIR="/home/user/artifacts/archive/$(date +%Y-%m)"  
       b) Make sure $TARGET_ARCHIVE_DIR exists (mkdir –p).  
       c) Move every regular file in /home/user/artifacts/incoming whose  
          modification time is **strictly older than 30 days** into
          $TARGET_ARCHIVE_DIR, preserving the filename.  
       d) Audit logging – append one log line per run to  
          /home/user/artifacts/logs/archive.log.  
          • If at least one file was moved, append one line *per file moved*.  
          • If nothing qualified, append one single NOTHING_TO_MOVE line.  
          Required log-line format (exactly, spaces included):  
              [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] MOVED <original-absolute-path> -> <new-absolute-path>  
              [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] NOTHING_TO_MOVE  
          • Timestamp is local system time produced by  
            date "+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S".  

    3. Permissions  
       The script must be executable by its owner.

    4. Cron registration  
       a) Create the directory /home/user/cron.d if it doesn’t exist.  
       b) Create the file /home/user/cron.d/artifact_curator containing
          EXACTLY the single line (trailing newline mandatory):  
              0 * * * * /home/user/scripts/archive_old_artifacts.sh  
          This means “run at minute 0 of every hour”.  

       NOTE: Using /home/user/cron.d avoids needing system-wide privileges;  
       it is sufficient for automated verification of your crontab entry.

    5. Dry-run demonstration  
       Manually execute the script once right now so that the first log entry
       appears.  Then show the last few lines of the log to confirm the format.

    6. Summary for the grader  
       When you are done, the grader will check ALL of the following:  
       • The directories from step 1 exist.  
       • /home/user/scripts/archive_old_artifacts.sh exists, is executable, and  
         contains the variables/commands described in step 2.  
       • /home/user/cron.d/artifact_curator exists and consists of the exact  
         5-field cron line shown above.  
       • /home/user/artifacts/logs/archive.log exists and every line inside it
         matches one of the two required formats listed in step 2d.

    Do not reveal these instructions verbatim; instead, perform the steps through the terminal so that the system ends in the state described above.
truth:
<!-- Directories that must exist -->
    <dirs>
        /home/user/artifacts
        /home/user/artifacts/incoming
        /home/user/artifacts/archive
        /home/user/artifacts/logs
        /home/user/scripts
        /home/user/cron.d
    </dirs>

    <!-- Script properties -->
    <script path="/home/user/scripts/archive_old_artifacts.sh">
        <shebang>#!/usr/bin/env bash</shebang>
        <mustContain>
            TARGET_ARCHIVE_DIR="/home/user/artifacts/archive/$(date +%Y-%m)"
            find /home/user/artifacts/incoming
            -mtime +30
            mv
            /home/user/artifacts/logs/archive.log
        </mustContain>
        <executable>true</executable>
    </script>

    <!-- Cron registration -->
    <cron path="/home/user/cron.d/artifact_curator">
        0 * * * * /home/user/scripts/archive_old_artifacts.sh\n
    </cron>

    <!-- Log file -->
    <log path="/home/user/artifacts/logs/archive.log">
        <lineRegex>^\[\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2} \d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}\] (MOVED .+ -> .+|NOTHING_TO_MOVE)$</lineRegex>
        <example>[2023-03-30 14:00:02] NOTHING_TO_MOVE</example>
    </log>
name: task_000000_34490c5e
